title: Azure Logic Apps - Monitoring, Observability, And Failure Alerting
applies_to: Monitoring daily Logic Apps data pipelines in production
Querying run history and diagnosing action failures via KQL
Setting up failure alerts for security data pipelines
Application Insights integration for Logic Apps workflows

confirmed_facts.fact: Enable diagnostics for Consumption Logic Apps: in the portal go to Diagnostics settings > Add diagnostic setting. Send WorkflowRuntime logs and AllMetrics to a Log Analytics workspace. Categories: WorkflowRuntime covers run start/end, action start/end, and trigger events.

Standard Logic Apps have built-in Application Insights support configured at resource creation. Enable or update via the portal under Settings > Application Insights or set APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING in app settings. Traces, dependencies, and custom events are emitted automatically.

KQL query to find failed workflow runs in Log Analytics: `AzureDiagnostics | where ResourceProvider == 'MICROSOFT.LOGIC' | where category == 'WorkflowRuntime' | where status_s == 'Failed' | project TimeGenerated, resource_workflowName_s, error_message_s, runId_s | order by TimeGenerated desc`.

KQL query to find failed actions within runs: `AzureDiagnostics | where ResourceProvider == 'MICROSOFT.LOGIC' | where category == 'WorkflowRuntime' | where OperationName == 'Microsoft.Logic/workflows/workflowActionCompleted' | where status_s == 'Failed' | project TimeGenerated, resource_actionName_s, error_code_s, error_message_s, runId_s`.

Run history retention: Consumption Logic Apps retain run history for 90 days in the portal. Standard Logic Apps retain history for 60 days by default, configurable up to 90 days via host.json `runHistoryRetentionInDays`. Log Analytics / App Insights retention is governed by the workspace retention setting (default 30 days, configurable to 730 days).

Create an Azure Monitor alert rule for Logic App failures: in the portal go to the Logic App > Alerts > New alert rule. Condition: metric 'Runs Failed' > 0, aggregation period 5 minutes. Action group: email, SMS, or webhook to Teams/Slack. This fires within minutes of a failed run without polling the run history API.

Available metrics for Consumption Logic Apps in Azure Monitor: Runs Started, Runs Completed, Runs Succeeded, Runs Failed, Runs Skipped, Runs Throttled, Action Latency, Trigger Latency, Billable Action Executions, Billable Trigger Executions. All metrics available with 1-minute granularity.

Add custom tracking properties to Logic Apps actions to improve log correlation: in the action properties, add a 'Tracked Properties' object with key-value pairs such as `{"correlationId": "@{triggerBody()?['id']}", "dataSource": "Qualys"}`. Tracked properties appear as custom dimensions in Application Insights and as columns in Log Analytics.

Query Logic Apps run duration distribution in KQL: `AzureDiagnostics | where ResourceProvider == 'MICROSOFT.LOGIC' | where OperationName == 'Microsoft.Logic/workflows/workflowRunCompleted' | extend durationMs = todouble(end_t - start_t) / 10000 | summarize avg(durationMs), percentiles(durationMs, 50, 95, 99) by resource_workflowName_s`.

implementation_notes: For security data pipelines that run daily, set up two alert rules: one for Runs Failed > 0 (immediate notification) and one for Runs Succeeded = 0 over a 25-hour window (catches silent non-starts).

Use a Log Analytics workspace shared across all related Logic Apps so cross-workflow correlation queries work — for example, correlating a Qualys extraction failure with a downstream SharePoint write failure.

Add a consistent 'pipelineRun' tracked property across all actions in a workflow. Set it to the trigger timestamp at the start. This lets you filter all logs for a specific execution with a single KQL filter.
For Standard Logic Apps, use the built-in monitoring dashboard in the portal (Overview > Runs history) for day-to-day operations. Use KQL in Log Analytics for trend analysis and alerting.
caveats: Diagnostics settings must be configured before data is sent to Log Analytics. Logs are not retroactively populated; historical runs before settings were enabled will not appear.
Consumption Logic App metrics are available in Azure Monitor immediately; Log Analytics ingestion has a 2-5 minute latency.
Standard Logic Apps in single-tenant mode emit telemetry in the OpenTelemetry format; queries may require slightly different KQL schemas compared to Consumption.
Run history in the portal is limited to 1000 runs for Consumption. For workflows with higher frequency, rely on Log Analytics for complete history.
